scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched against developer.drip.com's full endpoint index (accounts, single-email/email-series campaigns, custom fields, conversions/goals, events, forms, orders (legacy)/shopper-activity (v3), subscribers, tags, users, workflows/workflow-triggers, webhooks) plus the drip-ruby/drip-php SDK sources for exact request/response shapes. 7 read streams (the 4 pre-existing plus workflows/forms/webhooks) and 14 write actions covering subscriber/tag/event/broadcast/workflow-state/webhook lifecycle mutation. Excluded: email-series campaign activate/pause/subscribe (a distinct, more complex multi-step drip-sequence resource than the modeled workflow/broadcast shapes, and not part of legacy's original 4 streams), legacy orders + v3 shopper-activity batch endpoints (e-commerce event ingestion requiring a cart/product/order domain model this bundle does not otherwise touch), custom_field_identifiers/goals/event_actions (schema-introspection lists, not object data), users/me (authenticated-user identity, not account data), and the tags list endpoint (GET /tags returns a BARE JSON STRING ARRAY {"tags": ["a", "b"]}, which connsdk.RecordsAt silently yields ZERO records for — the exact same non-object-array-element gap documented for ip2whois's nameservers field in conventions.md §5 ledger item 12 — so tags are covered only via the apply_tag/remove_tag WRITE actions, which need no list extraction).

excluded.reason: single-subscriber detail; identical record shape to the subscribers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
per-subscriber campaign-membership sub-resource; a join view derivable from the subscribers/campaigns streams already synced, not a distinct top-level object

bulk multi-subscriber batch upsert over the same collection create_or_update_subscriber already covers per-record; this dialect's per-record write model already expresses the single-subscriber shape this endpoint's batch array wraps
bulk multi-subscriber batch unsubscribe; unsubscribe_subscriber already covers the single-subscriber shape this endpoint's batch array wraps

returns a BARE JSON STRING ARRAY ({"tags": ["Customer", "SEO"]}), not an array of objects; connsdk.RecordsAt only extracts array elements that decode as a JSON object, so every element here would be silently dropped, yielding zero records — the identical gap class documented for ip2whois's nameservers field (conventions.md ledger item 12), not a scoping choice. Tag mutation is still covered via apply_tag/remove_tag below, which operate per-subscriber and need no list extraction.
lists the names of custom fields ever set on any subscriber; schema/field-name introspection, not object data itself
conversion-goal definition list; marketing-analytics configuration, not core CRM/subscriber-shaped data prioritized this pass
single-conversion-goal detail; same scope narrowing as the goals list
bulk multi-event batch recording; record_event already covers the single-event shape this endpoint's batch array wraps
lists the distinct custom event action names ever recorded; schema/name introspection, not event object data itself
single-form detail; identical record shape to the forms list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
legacy e-commerce order-tracking API; superseded by the v3 shopper_activity endpoints and not part of a CRM-shaped record/mutation model this pass targets
legacy batch order-tracking; same scope narrowing as the single-order endpoint
e-commerce cart-activity ingestion; requires a cart/line-item domain model this connector's read streams do not otherwise expose or need
e-commerce order-activity ingestion; same domain-model scope narrowing as cart/batch
e-commerce product-activity ingestion; same domain-model scope narrowing as cart/batch
single-campaign detail; identical record shape to the campaigns list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

activates an email-series (drip-sequence) campaign; campaigns were not part of legacy's write surface and this multi-step-sequence resource is out of scope for this pass's write coverage, unlike the simpler single-broadcast and single-workflow state toggles this pass does cover
pauses an email-series campaign; same scope narrowing as activate
campaign-scoped filtered view of the same subscriber records the subscribers stream already reads in full
subscribes a person to an email-series campaign; same email-series-write scope narrowing as activate/pause
single-broadcast detail; identical record shape to the broadcasts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
sends a one-off test-send copy of a draft broadcast to a preview address; a preview/QA action, not a syncable record mutation
single-workflow detail; identical record shape to the workflows list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
starts a specific person on a workflow, a distinct enrollment action from the workflow-level activate/pause state toggles this pass covers; out of scope for this pass's write coverage
removes one person from a workflow; same per-enrollment scope narrowing as the subscribe endpoint
workflow-trigger configuration list; automation-config metadata, not CRM-shaped object data
creates a workflow trigger; automation-config mutation, out of scope for this pass
updates a workflow trigger; automation-config mutation, out of scope for this pass
single-webhook detail; identical record shape to the webhooks list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-account detail; identical record shape to the accounts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
authenticated-user identity introspection, not a syncable object stream
